page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-64 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It is configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced resource allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware profile of this instance is designed for workloads requiring substantial parallel processing capabilities without the need for specialized hardware acceleration or local NVMe storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute resources for multi-threaded applications. Qualitative cost efficiency is characterized by the high resource density of 64 dedicated cores, making it suitable for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications, web serving, and distributed database nodes that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-64 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4a server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute resources for multi-threaded workloads. This instance is designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads, web serving, and database nodes that utilize network-attached storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-64 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and no local storage or GPU accelerators, designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory for balanced enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads and web serving
